description: A static cache key serves stale dependencies forever, masking lockfile changes and hiding broken installs.
body: Failure mode: actions/cache uses a constant key (no lockfile hash), so once written the cache is restored on every run regardless of dependency changes, meaning a lockfile bump is never actually installed and CI tests a different dependency set than production. Detection signals: cache key with no hashFiles of the lockfile; dependency updates not reflected in CI; green CI but broken install on a clean checkout. Do not use constant cache keys for dependency caches. Safer fallback: key on a prefix plus hashFiles(lockfile) with restore-keys for partial hits, and verify the cache misses and reinstalls when the lockfile changes.
